mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls indicates robust economic output and stable labor demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive rank IC greater than 0.05 over any 24-month rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales and housing starts reflect immediate consumer demand strength, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns within the next 5 trading days during recovery phases.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative forward returns in over 60% of triggered cases during post-shock recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above trend reflects sustained aggregate demand expansion, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 against 60-day forward returns falls below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm labor market tightening, boosting near-term equity sentiment and generating positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative forward returns in over 55% of observations during late-cycle expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the index of industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output and supply-side contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days during downturns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of periods where INDPRO declines for two consecutive months during contraction regimes.
